name: Team Tomlinson
subject: “If you’re looking for leadership, here’s some.”
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TERESA TOMLINSON FOR SENATE
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sweet-briar-tt?refcode=em_header [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sweet-briar-tt?refcode=em_header]
Dear Friend –
Just four years ago Sweet Briar College faced a crisis and its board planned to close its doors forever. But this weekend, against all odds, the college will graduate it’s 110th all-women class of students, ready to go out and make their way in the world.
And that is thanks, in large part, to the woman who could be the next U.S. Senator from Georgia, with your help.
The Roanoke Times is the latest to extol Teresa Tomlinson’s leadership skills in the face of tremendous adversity — just this weekend they released a rare op-ed about the kind of Senator people can expect Teresa to be.
Their message to voters? “If you’re looking for leadership, here’s some.”
Our country needs more people like Teresa in the Senate who will do what she’s always done when faced with a challenge, injustice, or dysfunction: fight tirelessly to stand up for what’s right and get things done. Chip in now to support bringing Teresa’s extraordinary leadership to the US Senate. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sweet-briar-tt?refcode=em052019&noskip=true]
The op-ed lays out Teresa’s dogged efforts to build a massive coalition and save Sweet Briar College, her alma mater, in the face of near-certain closure. Teresa, along with the Sweet Briar Nation of strong and fierce women, built the coalition, established a new board, raised the millions needed, and the school never closed for a single day... an outcome the paper describes as “nothing short of miraculous.”
We could use some miraculous leadership in the U.S. Senate.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sweet-briar-tt?refcode=em052019&noskip=true [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sweet-briar-tt?refcode=em052019&noskip=true]
But this isn’t just about saving a college — this showed true leadership in the face of unthinkable odds.
“Tomlinson wasn’t just a figurehead,” the paper concludes. “She was hands-on – digging into the budget to make the numbers work.” Winning the battle, cleaning up the mess, and seeing that things prosper. That’s what Teresa Tomlinson does. And that is exactly what we need in our next senator from Georgia.
Chip in now, and let’s elect a leader we can count on to unite us to get things done. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sweet-briar-tt?refcode=em052019&noskip=true]
When the going gets tough, Teresa has shown time and time again that she will fight fearlessly for Georgians — just as she did as mayor, and as she did for her alma mater.
Onward!
Team TomlinsonDONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tt-perdue?refcode=em051819b&noskip=true]

Friend,
**At the beginning of 2019, Governor Holcomb said that this year’s legislative session would be about delivering practical, people-centered solutions for Hoosiers.**
_By the end of the legislative session, Governor Holcomb delivered on his promise by helping pass Indiana’s eighth-straight balanced budget – while successfully making historic investments in K-12 education funding!_
**The good news is Governor Holcomb isn’t even close to being done with delivering results for Hoosiers in 2019. He is committed to continuing to make progress on the Five Pillars of his Next Level Agenda for Hoosiers:**
\- Grow Indiana’s economy

\- Build Indiana’s infrastructure to connect Hoosiers to the world

\- Support Hoosiers with lifelong education & workforce development

\- Promote public health & attack the drug epidemic

\- Provide great government service to Hoosiers
Governor Holcomb’s Next Level Agenda is about making life better for Hoosiers, and that’s why he wants your input on the issues. **[Which issue area is most important to you? name: Kamala Harris
subject: This is a long email about closing the gender pay gap in America. I hope you’ll read it, then add your name to join me in this fight:
body:
My mother, Shyamala, had two goals in her life: to raise her two daughters
and end breast cancer. She was one of the first women of color to have a
position as a scientist at the University of California, Berkeley. As the
daughter of a working mother in a male-dominated field, I know the fight
to be treated equally in the workplace has persisted for generations.

Women who work full time in America are paid -- on average -- just 80
cents for every dollar paid to men. And for women of color, the gap is
even wider. For Latinas it’s 53 cents, for Native American women it’s 58
cents, and for Black women it’s 61 cents. This adds up to more than
$400,000 over the course of a woman’s career, and more than $1 million for
Latinas, Native women, and Black women.

We can fix this. Today, I’m proud to announce that our campaign is
unveiling a historic plan to help close the pay gap between women and men
by forcing corporations to be accountable for equal pay.

Our plan will finally put the burden of ensuring equal pay for equal work
on the corporations responsible for pay gaps between women and men -- not
the women employees who are experiencing discrimination.

We can ensure women earn the wages they deserve by forcing companies to
step up, holding them accountable when they don’t, and committing as a
nation to ending pay inequity once and for all.

Here’s how we’ll do it:

Companies will be required to obtain an “Equal Pay Certification” and prove
they’re not paying women less than men for work of equal value.

To receive certification, companies must demonstrate they have eliminated
pay disparities between women and men who are doing work of equal value.
To the extent pay disparities do exist for similar jobs, companies will be
required to show the gap is based on merit, performance, or seniority --
not gender.

But it’s not just unequal pay for similar work that drives the wage gap.
Too often, women are passed over for promotions, not hired for senior
roles, or are prevented from advancing due to the time they take off to
care for a new child or ailing parent. These are forms of systemic pay
discrimination too, and we need to shine a light on them.

That’s why under our plan, companies will be required to report statistics
on the percentage of women in leadership positions and the percentage of
women who are amongst the company’s top earners. They will also be
required to report the overall pay and total compensation gap that exists
between men and women, regardless of job titles, experience, and
performance. These statistics will be reported by employees’ race and
ethnicity.

Companies will be fined 1% of their profits for every 1% wage gap they allow
to persist for work of equal value.

Companies that fail to receive “Equal Pay Certification” will face a fine
for every day they discriminate against their workers. For every 1% gap
that exists after accounting for differences in job titles, experience,
and performance, companies will be fined at 1% of their average daily
profits during the last fiscal year.

Our plan is estimated to generate roughly $180 billion over 10 years.

Fines will be invested in building on universal paid family and medical leave.

We must address the systemic inequalities that drive the pay gap,
including the wage penalty women pay when caring for a new child or a sick
parent. On average, women receive a 4% pay cut for each child they have,
compared to men who receive a 6% pay increase. The lack of paid leave --
for women and men -- is a major driver of the wage penalty.

America is the only industrialized nation in the world that fails to
guarantee our workers any type of paid family and medical leave. That
needs to change. That’s why, as president, I will fight for the FAMILY Act
to provide workers with up to 12 weeks of paid family and medical leave.
Fines collected under our plan will help build on the FAMILY Act,
increasing the percentage of wages workers receive when taking time to
care for themselves or a loved one.

Companies will be required to disclose whether they are “Equal Pay Certified”
on the homepage of their websites.

Compliance reports will be posted publicly on Equal Employment Opportunity
Commission’s (EEOC) website. These reports will empower individual
employees to assess where they fall on their company’s pay scale and as in
the UK, allow the public to hold corporations accountable for pay gaps.

To ensure equality in all workplaces, we’ll overhaul anti-discrimination laws
and expand investigations of complaints to secure justice for victims of
discrimination.

The wage gap isn’t just a number: It’s the countless women across America
who have been the target of discrimination. We need to support them in
every workplace, no matter the size. Under our plan, we’ll significantly
strengthen and expand anti-discrimination protections to ensure all
workers, no matter the size of their employer, are covered by Title VII of
the Civil Rights Act.

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/go/10046?t=1001&akid=4913%2E5755789%2EkR6kc6 ]READ THE FULL PLAN

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-email-petition-190519-equal-pay?refcode=ema190520-equalpay-full&source=ema190520-equalpay-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=1002&akid=4913%2E5755789%2EkR6kc6 ]I’m calling on you to join me today. Add your name next to mine to
support our campaign’s plan to help close the wage gap between men and
women in our country.

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-email-petition-190519-equal-pay?refcode=ema190520-equalpay-full&source=ema190520-equalpay-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=1003&akid=4913%2E5755789%2EkR6kc6 ]ADD MY NAME

The wage gap between men and women in our country has barely budged this
entire century, and the burden for ensuring equal pay has often fallen on
workers -- requiring them to hold employers accountable for pay
discrimination through costly lawsuits that are increasingly difficult to
prove. This is wrong.

Together, we can flip the script by placing the burden squarely on the
corporations responsible for pay inequity and radically change the way we
enforce equal pay in America.

Thanks for joining me in support of our new plan to help close the wage
gap.

For The People,

— Kamala Harris

Kamala Harris has spent her entire life defending our American values.
From fighting to fix our broken criminal justice system to taking on the
Wall Street Banks for middle-class homeowners, Kamala has always worked
For The People.

We’re
counting on all of our supporters to step off the sidelines and get into
this fight. Will you add your first donation to Kamala’s campaign
today?

name: Phil Bredesen
subject: Why I'm Supporting James
disclaimer_text: Paid for by James Mackler for U
body:
I'm writing to tell you that Andrea and I are supporting James Mackler for the US Senate seat that will be decided in November 2020.
|
---
[ ](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa2ad/18d6a294/4a576f7f/26d69f12/1618173232/VEsH/ "https://www.jamesmackler.com/")

| | Friend, **I 'm writing to tell you that Andrea and I are supporting James Mackler for the US Senate seat** that will be decided in November 2020. This is the open seat that Sen. Alexander is vacating. I got to know James well during my own race for the Senate last year. He had begun campaigning for that seat himself and graciously stepped aside when I entered the race. He then did everything he could to support me; he was at my events, he spoke on my behalf, he offered excellent advice. He was a class act in every way. I shouldn't have been surprised: remember that this is a man who enlisted in the US Army after 9/11 because he felt he needed to do something for his country. As a 30-year old attorney, he closed his practice, got an age waiver, learned to fly a Black Hawk helicopter, and became a _Screaming Eagle_ in the 101st Airborne Division. That kind of stepping forward, when no one would have expected you to do so, is true selflessness and love of country. We need a lot more of it in Washington. With James' background, his genuine personality and his likeability, he will be an excellent candidate and a man Tennessee can be proud to send to Washington. [**_Finally, money: Andrea and I have each given the legal maximum to his campaign --$5,600 each. This is the open seat that Sen. Alexander is vacating. I got to know James well during my own race for the Senate last year. He had begun campaigning for that seat himself and graciously stepped aside when I entered the race. He then did everything he could to support me; he was at my events, he spoke on my behalf, he offered excellent advice. He was a class act in every way. I shouldn't have been surprised: remember that this is a man who enlisted in the US Army after 9/11 because he felt he needed to do something for his country. As a 30-year old attorney, he closed his practice, got an age waiver, learned to fly a Black Hawk helicopter, and became a Screaming Eagle in the 101st Airborne Division. That kind of stepping forward, when no one would have expected you to do so, is true selflessness and love of country. We need a lot more of it in Washington. body:
[1]Beto for America

This campaign is not just about electing Beto president. Now that may
sound odd, but hear us out for a second!

The truth is that no president can tackle the challenges we face on their
own. Only this country can do that, and only if we build a movement that
includes all of us.

So when we send out an email asking people to donate, we’re not just
counting how much money we’ve raised. What’s far more important to us is
the number of people stepping up each time to own a piece of this
grassroots campaign.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17564?refcode=ema20190520-31906&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1019&akid=31906%2E444211%2EO642Ei ]With that in mind, we are setting an important goal of reaching 25,000
new donations by the end of the month. Can you chip in $3

It’s a way of encouraging other people to step up too. We know that no one

subject: Trump’s Immigration Plan Recognizes That We Urgently Need Workers – Here’s Why It Could Work
body:
"Our current immigration system is broken and doesn’t meet the needs of the American people." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7b94375712&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Trump’s Immigration Plan Recognizes That We Urgently Need Workers – Here’s Why It Could Work**

“Our current immigration system is broken and doesn’t meet the needs of the American people. These laws were [written in] the 1960s for an America and an American economy that, quite frankly, no longer exist,” Adam Brandon writes in Fox News.

“An aging workforce and lower birthrates should be a concern. Fewer workers mean lower productivity. Lower productivity means less economic output. Less economic output means a nation in decline . . . President Trump’s plan resets the conversation on immigration.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2d0dbc11e6&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| With their obstruction, Congressional Democrats risk killing off the improvements that President Trump’s U.S.–Mexico–Canada Agreement makes to the outdated NAFTA, economist Peter Morici writes in _[MarketWatch](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b71bafb4f8&e=3ee4985eea)_. “NAFTA was implemented in 1994 — long before the digital revolution, the mapping of DNA and biologicals, and Chinese mercantilism so fundamentally threatened North American jobs . . . America’s chief negotiator, Robert Lighthizer deserves high marks for his accomplishments in the new U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement.”
---
|
---
| “There’s no mystery about it. Policy affects progress, either by accelerating it or by putting limits on it,” _[The Washington Times](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a6154f186a&e=3ee4985eea)_ editorial board writes. “The latter was the story of the Obama years, when federal spending failed to stimulate the economy, as promised, and a prolonged and extensive regulatory binge chased American companies and jobs overseas.”
---
|
---
| “From now on, the Trump-Russia affair, the investigation that dominated the first years of Donald Trump's presidency, will be divided into two parts: before and after the release of the Mueller report,” Byron York writes in the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=eee54eb43d&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Before the release, many Democrats adopted a ‘wait for Mueller’ stance, basing their anti-Trump strategy on the hope that Mueller would find the much-anticipated conspiracy. Then Mueller did not deliver.”
---
|
---
| “Democrats have stooped to a new low in their witch-hunt against President Trump. Last week, House Judiciary Chairman Jerry Nadler, D-N.Y., led his committee's Democratic members to vote to hold Attorney General William Barr in contempt of Congress,” Rep. Daniel Webster (R-FL) writes in the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f924b3ac5c&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Democrats are making a mockery of our congressional oversight authority and committee proceedings. The American people deserve better.”
---

body:
| | Friend --

I've been working with Andrew since the early days when we operated out of an apartment. We knew we had a real shot to win this election then. Today, thanks to you, we’re more confident than ever. Last week, Andrew emailed you about [our new goal of raising $3.5 million by the next FEC reporting deadline on June 30th](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_4EuiRHvEQu_OzjBiZlAGzu1zYxK2yCvERX0viIJCtGKJ0m5vhAlbMYDmfe2HawpCQ&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05202019_updated_goal_2&n=2 "ActBlue Page for Andrew Yang"). This goal is everything. And before I ask you to donate to help us hit it—I'm writing today to tell you why. To date, our campaign has outperformed at every level: _They told us we were a longer than longshot. Then, we were one of the first Democrats to qualify for the debate stage with 65,000 donors._ _They told us we were just an internet fad, but we started drawing some of the largest crowds in the race._ _They told us we were a fringe candidate that could never compete in such a crowded field, yet we've cracked 3% polling in NH and SC._ So how do we continue to outperform? Well, like our candidate, our team is full of entrepreneurs and operators, and we have a plan. **Our plan is to show exponential growth in Q2 and create a dark-horse narrative that shows Andrew can actually win this whole election.** This is where you come in. We need your help to fund this narrative. We raised $1.7 million in the first quarter. All from people like you. And with your help, we're going to double that to $3.5 million in the second quarter. When we succeed, imagine the headlines: _"2020 candidate Andrew Yang DOUBLES his Q1 fundraising with $3.5M haul”_ _“Political newcomer Andrew Yang raises $3.5M — and threatens to upend 2020 Democratic race”_ _“Yang emerges as potential dark horse with $3.5M fundraising haul”_ _“Andrew Yang raises $3.5M in Q2—DOUBLE what he raised in Q1. Could he surprise the field and win the nomination?”_ That is the narrative we must create. We must show we can build the warchest necessary to compete against the top tier—and that we intend to win. That is what we need your help with. We’ve already raised $1,153,862 so far—we have just under $2.4 million more to go before June 30th. June 30th") **My****promise****to you as campaign manager is that we will do our part and run the most efficient, scrappy, and innovative campaign in election history.** Other candidates will raise tens of millions and waste it on bad advertisements and political consultants. Give us $3.5 million this quarter and we will contend. [**In return, my****ask****is that you'll help us finance it with any donation you can. And for the next six weeks, make it your top priority to grow and mobilize the Yang Gang to get us there by June 30th.**](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_4EuiRHvEQu_OzjBiZlAGzsGtFLOcViXsmAGEZhz7qtC&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05202019_updated_goal_2&n=4 "ActBlue Page for Andrew Yang") So this is the plan. We will not hide from the plan, we will own it, and we even have a new progress bar on the website for the world to see. [](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_4EuiRHvEQu_OzjBiZlAGzv4Rt5u5hydre7klhX6oVotWTlBqfuqeCY6nyyyO-RUvg&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05202019_updated_goal_2&n=5 "ActBlue Page for Andrew Yang") If everyone on this email list donates $20 today, we will hit the goal right away. This is our moment, Yang Gang. [**Can you step up today and give $20 to create our dark horse narrative?**](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_4EuiRHvEQu_OzjBiZlAGzsGtFLOcViXsmAGEZhz7qtC&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05202019_updated_goal_2&n=6 "ActBlue Page for Andrew Yang") Thank you all for what you’ve done so far. Our team will be working day and night to reach this goal. Let’s be the dark horse of 2020. Humanity First, Zach

Three days ago, Elizabeth announced her new plan to protect abortion rights across the country.

**And we need to fight to make it happen -- as soon as possible.**

The Missouri state legislature passed an unconstitutional abortion ban. And the governor is expected to sign it into law as soon as today.

**Elizabeth 's plan would sweep away state-level bans, guarantee the rights enshrined in Roe v. Wade even if the Supreme Court overturns it, and go further to ensure reproductive justice nationwide.**

**[Tens of thousands of people have already signed on to support it. But according to our records, you haven't yet. Will you add your name now?](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fb8f/45c30650/9ed257b2/66996aed/262985317/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDUyMGVtMS12MXMxIn0=/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/protect-roe-v-wade?source=20190520em1-v1s1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Team Warren

\---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: **Elizabeth Warren** <info@elizabethwarren.com>
Date: Fri, May 17, 2019
Subject: Even if the Supreme Court overturns or chips away at Roe v. Wade, Congress can still protect abortion rights and ensure reproductive justice. Here's how:
To: dpwillis67@gmail.com
---
In Alabama, Missouri, and in states across the country, extremist Republican lawmakers are trying to turn back the clock, outlaw abortion, and deny women access to reproductive health care.

Even though _Roe v. Wade_ established a woman's constitutional right to a safe and legal abortion -- and has been the law of the land for over 46 years -- these extremist Republican lawmakers don't care. And they are hoping the Supreme Court will back their radical play.

And it just might work. Donald Trump has packed the courts with extreme, anti-choice judges. Senate Republicans stole a Supreme Court seat and rammed through the confirmation of Brett Kavanaugh last year to cement an anti-choice majority on the Supreme Court.

**This is a dark moment. People are scared and angry. And they are right to be. But this isn 't a moment to back down -- it's time to fight back.

And I've got a plan.

Congress should pass new federal laws that protect access to reproductive care from right-wing ideologues in the states. Federal laws that ensure real access to birth control and abortion care for all women. Federal laws that will stand no matter what the Supreme Court does.

[Add your name if you agree, and let's fight to get this done.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fb8f/45c30650/9ed257b2/66996aed/262985317/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDUyMGVtMS12MXMxIn0=/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/protect-roe-v-wade?source=20190520em1-v1s1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Here's what our plan looks like:

**1\. Create federal, statutory rights that parallel the constitutional right in _Roe v. Wade_.**

These rights would have at least two key components. First, they must prohibit states from interfering in the ability of a health care provider to provide medical care, including abortion services. Second, they must prohibit states from interfering in the ability of a patient to access medical care, including abortion services, from a provider that offers them.

Federal law preempts state law, so as soon as Congress takes this step, we'd sweep away the bans in Alabama, Georgia, Ohio, and anywhere else.

We'd end the political games being played by right-wing courts to try to narrow _Roe_ 's protections. And because these federal protections would be valid on a variety of constitutional grounds, they would ensure that choice would remain the law of the land even if the Supreme Court overturns _Roe_.

**2\. Pass federal laws to preempt state efforts that functionally limit access to reproductive health care.**

States have passed countless "TRAP" laws - laws that circumvent _Roe_ to functionally limit and eliminate women's access to abortion care - which are are medically unnecessary and exist for only one purpose: to functionally eliminate the ability of women to access abortion services.

A bill already proposed in Congress, The Women's Health Protection Act, could help block these kinds of schemes concocted to deny women access to care. Congress should pass it.

**3\. Guarantee reproductive health coverage as part of all health coverage.**

All women -- no matter where they live, where they're from, how much money they make, or the color of their skin -- are entitled to access the high-quality, evidence-based reproductive health care that is envisioned by _Roe_.

Making that a reality starts with repealing the Hyde Amendment, which blocks abortion coverage for women under federally funded health care programs like Medicaid, the VA, and the Indian Health Service.

Congress should also expand culturally and linguistically appropriate services and information and include immigrant women in conversations about coverage and access. Congress must also pass the EACH Woman Act, which would also prohibit abortion restrictions on private insurance. And we should ensure that all future health coverage -- including Medicare for All -- includes contraception and abortion coverage.

**Ensure equal access and reproductive justice.**

We must undo the current administration's efforts to undermine women's access to reproductive health care -- including ending Trump's gag rule -- and fully support Title X family planning funding. We must crack down on violence at abortion clinics and ensure that women are not discriminated against at work or anywhere else for the choices they made about their bodies.

And these issues are bigger than _Roe_. The women of color who have championed the reproductive justice movement teach us that we must go beyond choice to ensure meaningful access for every woman in America -- not just the privileged and wealthy few.

We must go beyond abortion, to ensure access to contraception, STI prevention and care, comprehensive sex education, care for pregnant moms, safe home and work environments, adequate wages, and so much more. We must build a future that protects the right of all women to have children, the right of all women to not have children, and the right to bring children up in a safe and healthy environment.

**The overwhelming majority of Americans have no desire to return to the world before _Roe v. Wade_. And so the time to act is now.**

It's inspiring to see so many women coming off the sidelines in this fight -- and women must continue to speak up to make sure this conversation stays grounded in their real experiences. Men must speak up too.

And Americans outraged by these efforts should get into the political arena, run for office, and make these right-wing Republican lawmakers face the consequences of their actions.

We're going through a tough time. A _really_ tough time. But we're not helpless. We're strong. We've got power in our democracy. So let's use it.

**Our democracy should not be held hostage by right-wing courts, and women should not have to hope that Brett Kavanaugh and Donald Trump 's Supreme Court will respect the law.

[Add your name if you agree Congress should act to ensure access to reproductive care -- including real access to birth control and abortion.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fb8f/45c30650/9ed257b2/66996aed/262985317/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDUyMGVtMS12MXMxIn0=/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/protect-roe-v-wade?source=20190520em1-v1s1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth

Here's how: To: dpwillis67@gmail.com In Alabama, Missouri, and in states across the country, extremist Republican lawmakers are trying to turn back the clock, outlaw abortion, and deny women access to reproductive health care. Even though Roe v. Wade established a woman's constitutional right to a safe and legal abortion -- and has been the law of the land for over 46 years -- these extremist Republican lawmakers don't care. And they are hoping the Supreme Court will back their radical play. And it just might work. Donald Trump has packed the courts with extreme, anti-choice judges. Senate Republicans stole a Supreme Court seat and rammed through the confirmation of Brett Kavanaugh last year to cement an anti-choice majority on the Supreme Court. This is a dark moment. People are scared and angry. And they are right to be. But this isn 't a moment to back down -- it's time to fight back. And I've got a plan. Congress should pass new federal laws that protect access to reproductive care from right-wing ideologues in the states. Federal laws that ensure real access to birth control and abortion care for all women. Federal laws that will stand no matter what the Supreme Court does. "" Here's what our plan looks like: 1\. Create federal, statutory rights that parallel the constitutional right in Roe v. Wade. These rights would have at least two key components. First, they must prohibit states from interfering in the ability of a health care provider to provide medical care, including abortion services. Second, they must prohibit states from interfering in the ability of a patient to access medical care, including abortion services, from a provider that offers them. Federal law preempts state law, so as soon as Congress takes this step, we'd sweep away the bans in Alabama, Georgia, Ohio, and anywhere else. We'd end the political games being played by right-wing courts to try to narrow Roe 's protections. And because these federal protections would be valid on a variety of constitutional grounds, they would ensure that choice would remain the law of the land even if the Supreme Court overturns Roe. 2\. Pass federal laws to preempt state efforts that functionally limit access to reproductive health care. States have passed countless "TRAP" laws - laws that circumvent Roe to functionally limit and eliminate women's access to abortion care - which are are medically unnecessary and exist for only one purpose: to functionally eliminate the ability of women to access abortion services. A bill already proposed in Congress, The Women's Health Protection Act, could help block these kinds of schemes concocted to deny women access to care. Congress should pass it. 3\. Guarantee reproductive health coverage as part of all health coverage. All women -- no matter where they live, where they're from, how much money they make, or the color of their skin -- are entitled to access the high-quality, evidence-based reproductive health care that is envisioned by Roe. Making that a reality starts with repealing the Hyde Amendment, which blocks abortion coverage for women under federally funded health care programs like Medicaid, the VA, and the Indian Health Service. Congress should also expand culturally and linguistically appropriate services and information and include immigrant women in conversations about coverage and access. Congress must also pass the EACH Woman Act, which would also prohibit abortion restrictions on private insurance. And we should ensure that all future health coverage -- including Medicare for All -- includes contraception and abortion coverage. Ensure equal access and reproductive justice. We must undo the current administration's efforts to undermine women's access to reproductive health care -- including ending Trump's gag rule -- and fully support Title X family planning funding. We must crack down on violence at abortion clinics and ensure that women are not discriminated against at work or anywhere else for the choices they made about their bodies. And these issues are bigger than Roe. The women of color who have championed the reproductive justice movement teach us that we must go beyond choice to ensure meaningful access for every woman in America -- not just the privileged and wealthy few. We must go beyond abortion, to ensure access to contraception, STI prevention and care, comprehensive sex education, care for pregnant moms, safe home and work environments, adequate wages, and so much more. We must build a future that protects the right of all women to have children, the right of all women to not have children, and the right to bring children up in a safe and healthy environment. The overwhelming majority of Americans have no desire to return to the world before Roe v. Wade. And so the time to act is now. It's inspiring to see so many women coming off the sidelines in this fight -- and women must continue to speak up to make sure this conversation stays grounded in their real experiences. Men must speak up too. And Americans outraged by these efforts should get into the political arena, run for office, and make these right-wing Republican lawmakers face the consequences of their actions. We're going through a tough time. A really tough time. But we're not helpless. We're strong. We've got power in our democracy. So let's use it. Our democracy should not be held hostage by right-wing courts, and women should not have to hope that Brett Kavanaugh and Donald Trump 's Supreme Court will respect the law. "" Thanks for being a part of this, Elizabeth "" | | | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" ---|---|---|---|---|--- | Paid for by Warren for President All content (C) 2019 Warren for President, All Rights Reserved PO Box 171375, Boston, MA 02116

| | | **Virginia United** is a new organization from State Senator Jennifer McClellan dedicated to securing a Democratic majority in the General Assembly so that Virginians in every corner of the Commonwealth can see progress.

**Will you consider** [**making a donation**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1776/3e0906/7b3028aa/3e308a00/3474913946/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/virginiaunited-kaine?source=20190520_TK_FR_Virginia-United&amount=30&refcode=20190520_TK_FR_Virginia-United&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=1+-+making+a+donation&utm_campaign=20190520_1_FR_TK_Virginia-United") **to support her efforts today?** | ******[**DONATE**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1776/3e0906/7b3028aa/3e308a07/3474913946/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/virginiaunited-kaine?source=20190520_TK_FR_Virginia-United&amount=30&refcode=20190520_TK_FR_Virginia-United&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=2+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=20190520_1_FR_TK_Virginia-United")******
---
Thank you,

Derek:

Our great Commonwealth of Virginia has made truly incredible progress in the past decade. I've called it the best political turnaround project in the United States from red to blue -- **_but the truth is, we're not quite blue yet._**

[**This November, we've got another shot at taking a Democratic majority in the state legislature and showing once and for all that Virginia is united around opportunity for all.**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1776/3e0906/7b3028aa/3e308a06/3474913946/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/virginiaunited-kaine?source=20190520_TK_FR_Virginia-United&amount=30&refcode=20190520_TK_FR_Virginia-United&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=3+-+This+November+weve+got+another+shot+at+t&utm_campaign=20190520_1_FR_TK_Virginia-United") I already know that's the case -- I've traveled to every corner of this Commonwealth and talked to Virginians in big cities and small towns, men and women, parents and kids, oyster farmers and tech workers and veterans -- and no matter who I'm talking to, I hear the same things. Folks want safe neighborhoods, fair-paying jobs, quality education, affordable health care, and welcoming communities.

The great Democrats running for our General Assembly are ready to fight for all of that. So what we need to do is make sure that voters in every ZIP code in Virginia know who those Democrats are, and when and how to go vote for them. **My friend, State Senator Jennifer McClellan, is stepping up with a new organization called Virginia United that's dedicated to doing just that. Will you join her and make a donation to Virginia United right now?**

Voters in Virginia have shown that there is a true and lasting path to victory for strong, problem-solving, forward-thinking Democrats. They've voted for Democrats in the past three presidential elections, the past two gubernatorial elections, and in every statewide race for the last 10 years. There's a lot to be proud of.

But throughout all that, the Virginia General Assembly has remained under Republican leadership. Democrats serving in the House of Delegates and Virginia Senate have seen their progressive legislation languish in Republican-led committees. They've pushed and pushed for wins like ratifying the Equal Rights Amendment and securing fair housing for LGBTQ Virginians, and they've fallen short by just a couple of votes. This November, we can change that.

**Together, we can help Virginians unite around our shared values. Thank you for joining Jennifer McClellan and me in this effort.**

_-Tim_

Will you consider "" to support her efforts today? | **** ""** Thank you, Derek: Our great Commonwealth of Virginia has made truly incredible progress in the past decade. I've called it the best political turnaround project in the United States from red to blue -- but the truth is, we're not quite blue yet. "" I already know that's the case -- I've traveled to every corner of this Commonwealth and talked to Virginians in big cities and small towns, men and women, parents and kids, oyster farmers and tech workers and veterans -- and no matter who I'm talking to, I hear the same things. Folks want safe neighborhoods, fair-paying jobs, quality education, affordable health care, and welcoming communities. The great Democrats running for our General Assembly are ready to fight for all of that. So what we need to do is make sure that voters in every ZIP code in Virginia know who those Democrats are, and when and how to go vote for them. My friend, State Senator Jennifer McClellan, is stepping up with a new organization called Virginia United that's dedicated to doing just that. Will you join her and make a donation to Virginia United right now? If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" Voters in Virginia have shown that there is a true and lasting path to victory for strong, problem-solving, forward-thinking Democrats. They've voted for Democrats in the past three presidential elections, the past two gubernatorial elections, and in every statewide race for the last 10 years. There's a lot to be proud of. But throughout all that, the Virginia General Assembly has remained under Republican leadership. Democrats serving in the House of Delegates and Virginia Senate have seen their progressive legislation languish in Republican-led committees. They've pushed and pushed for wins like ratifying the Equal Rights Amendment and securing fair housing for LGBTQ Virginians, and they've fallen short by just a couple of votes. This November, we can change that. Together, we can help Virginians unite around our shared values. Thank you for joining Jennifer McClellan and me in this effort. -Tim ** ""** | "" "" ""

| | **FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE**
**Contact:** Pete Seat, 520-990-4067, pseat@indiana.gop **Date:** May 20, 2019 **House District 93 Caucus Set**
_Caucus set to choose replacement for Representative David Frizzell_ **INDIANAPOLIS** \- Indiana Republican Party Chairman Kyle Hupfer officially called a caucus of eligible precinct committee members to fill the upcoming vacancy in the office of House District 93. The seat is currently held by Rep. David Frizzell, whose resignation from the Indiana House of Representatives will be effective May 31, 2019. The caucus will be held at 6:30 p.m. ET on Tuesday, June 4, 2019, at the Perry Township Government Center at 4925 Shelby St., Indianapolis, IN 46227. Chairman Hupfer received official communication from the Indiana House of Representatives concerning Rep. David Frizzell’s resignation on May 2. The individual selected at the June 4 caucus will fill the remainder of Rep. Frizzell’s term. **“Throughout David Frizzell’s 27 years of service in the House, Indiana has transformed from a middle-of-the-pack state to a national leader in balanced budgets and responsible government,"** said Hupfer. **"Hoosiers in Marion and Johnson counties will definitely miss Rep. Frizzell's service."** Individuals interested in running in the caucus should contact the Indiana Republican Party Secretary at [dzagone@indiana.gop](mailto:dzagone@indiana.gop) to ensure they file the proper forms prior to the deadline, which is 72 hours prior to the vote. The caucus will be open to credentialed media who pre-register to Pete Seat at [p](mailto:pseat@indiana.gop)[seat@indiana.gop](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f052a51171&e=1acd9bb3d6) prior to 5:00 p.m. ET on Monday, June 3.

A Facebook Live Stream will be available at [www.facebook.com/indgop](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=4586412e40&e=1acd9bb3d6) for members of the public who wish to watch the caucus live. More details regarding caucus procedures and candidates will be released after the filing deadline. **###**

name: Abigail (personal)
subject: I’m the first woman elected in Virginia’s 7th district, but I won’t be the last
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger For Congress
body:
I'm proud to be serving with the most diverse Congress in our nation's history and to be working alongside a record 101 other women in the House of Representatives.
| | Peter,

I was the _first_ woman voters of VA-07 ever elected to represent our district in Congress -- but I know I won't be the last. As one writer from _Richmond Magazine[1]_ so aptly explains, **" Virginia's Future Is Female."**

Take one look at the 116th Congress, and it's easy to see that what happened in Virginia didn't happen in isolation. **I 'm proud to be serving with the most diverse Congress in our nation's history** and to be working alongside a **record 101 other women in the House of Representatives.**

The value of bringing new voices to Capitol Hill is clear in our first five months in office. From voting on legislation to protect the civil rights of LGBTQ individuals, to passing a strenghtened Violence Against Women Act, to cosponsoring legislation to lower the prescriptions prices for senior citizens, to voting for a comprehensive anti-discrimination resolution, to helping launch the first-ever bipartisan caucus focused on the needs of women veterans, I am working hard to be a voice for all people in the 7th and across the country.

**Peter -- I worked tirelessly in 2018 to win a seat at the table and to be a voice for others. I 'm proud of all we have accomplished, and I intend to keep working on behalf of my constituents and our country. [Could you help ensure I remain seated at the table, so I can welcome more women and diverse voices to the conversation? >>](https://act.myngp.com/el/RiO_qXjIWfoMb61ENGoI7EeXOQ1rOjWS7KnrC0OaSiw=/KVy1CFLWVZQ2FOJr4K4DBkZZjs5NnvC2Z5ii5rX5LpA=?refcode=em_fr_2019.05.20_b1_as_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800)** | [CONTRIBUTE »](https://act.myngp.com/el/RiO_qXjIWfoMb61ENGoI7EeXOQ1rOjWS7KnrC0OaSiw=/KVy1CFLWVZQ2FOJr4K4DBkZZjs5NnvC2Z5ii5rX5LpA=?refcode=em_fr_2019.05.20_b1_as_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800)
---
Help me show the nation that our new generation of leaders are here to stay.

Thank you for your support,

_Abigail_

body:
Do you think I deserve to share my unique story and vision in the debates? >> | | |
---
| |
---
| | | This is the most important question I've ever asked you: The threshold for future Democratic debates is going to be even tougher. **Do you think I deserve to share my unique story and vision in the debates?** **[_YES >>_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd1a/3eb3e2fc/4f42e357/74498b54/3790054209/VEsH/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/julian-priorities-survey?source=20190520_em_pet_b1_castro_all") [_NO >>_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd1a/3eb3e2fc/4f42e357/74498b54/3790054209/VEsE/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/julian-priorities-survey?source=20190520_em_pet_b1_castro_all")**
---

People who grew up where I did...

Who were raised by single moms like I was...

Who come from a family of immigrants like I do...

We're not supposed to run for President -- and we're certainly not supposed to be frontrunners.

**But I want kids from all walks of life to know that -- no matter where you come from -- you can be President, too.**

Which is why sharing my story and vision in every Democratic debate is so personally important to me.

[_**But the threshold to qualify for future debates is going to be even tougher than the first two. Will you take my quick poll if you think I deserve to share my unique story on that national stage? >>**_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcd1a/3eb3e2fc/4f42e357/74498b54/3790054209/VEsF/ "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/julian-priorities-survey?source=20190520_em_pet_b1_castro_all")

Thank you,

Julian Castro

subject: America is finally writing the rules on trade
body:
President Donald J. Trump delivered a major announcement just before the weekend about our two biggest trading partners | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=53a01e5ff8&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • May 20, 2019
---
| | |
# **America is finally writing the rules on trade**

**President Donald J. Trump** delivered a major announcement just before the weekend about our two biggest trading partners. “We’ve just reached an agreement with Canada and Mexico, and we’ll be selling our product into those countries without the imposition of tariffs or major tariffs,” the President said.
**🎬****Watch:**[President Trump announces a new trade milestone](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=caad3c80c8&e=3ee4985eea)
**The United States** has strong relationships with both Canada and Mexico, President Trump said—but that shouldn't stop us from negotiating hard to protect American workers and jobs. Canada, for example, has been “charging us extremely high tariffs, as much as 285 percent or more, for our agricultural products, which is an absolute barrier,” he said.

**In the past,** American Presidents would turn the other cheek on bad trade deals to avoid confrontation or criticism from the mainstream press. But unlike his predecessors, President Trump is focused on results for our farmers and manufacturers—not on praise from the Beltway or global media. The same day he announced that Canada and Mexico would lift their retaliatory tariffs, the Administration also reported that it had successfully negotiated a deal to remove Japan’s longstanding restrictions on American beef exports.

**All told** , these agreements are great news for American farmers. They will continue to protect America’s crucial steel and aluminum industries, too.

**Friday’s news** coincided with a Proclamation from President Trump recognizing World Trade Week, 2019, in which he reflected on the [trade principles he promised](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b2750cf8b8&e=3ee4985eea) to the White House:
| _"The American people see through Chairman Nadler’s desperate ploy to distract from the President’s historically successful agenda and our booming economy. Neither the White House nor Attorney General Barr will comply with Chairman Nadler’s unlawful and reckless demands . . . The American people deserve a Congress that is focused on solving real problems like the crisis at the border, high prescription drug prices, our country’s crumbling infrastructure, and so much more."_
---

**After decades of politicians** putting the global business class ahead of America’s industrial and agricultural heartland, working families finally heard the voice of a President that was willing to fight for them on January 20, 2017.

**“From this moment** on, it’s going to be America First,” President Trump said at his Inauguration. “Every decision on trade, on taxes, on immigration, on foreign affairs, will be made to benefit American workers and American families.”

[America announces deal with Canada and Mexico to lift retaliatory tariffs.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b6d2660b2e&e=3ee4985eea)
|
---
|
## **Video of the day: Who said it?**
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2d17ad7d24&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **Years before President Trump** took office, Sen. Chuck Schumer (D-NY) all but endorsed the type of immigration plan that President Trump unveiled last week.

**“The current status quo** on immigration makes no sense. We turn away people from entering the country who could create thousands of jobs and let people cross our borders who take away jobs,” Sen. Schumer said in 2013.

**Last week,** [President Trump unveiled an immigration plan](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d465abc5fa&e=3ee4985eea) that tackles that very problem—and wouldn’t you believe it, Sen. Schumer immediately rejected it. House Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-CA) did her part by calling the proposal “dead on arrival.”

**Where’s the old** Chuck Schumer when you need him?
---
|
---
|

subject: re: Climate Action

body:
| |
---
| Team, today Michael announced his ambitious climate action plan and his commitment to using executive action if Congress fails to act.

Check out his original message and sign the petition if you're ready to take action!
---

| [SIGN THE PETITION ->](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cba2e8/134673f2/39f5ca0/6996ec31/1080707450/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.michaelbennet.com/Climate-X-Option?source=EM_PET_B2_052019_AC&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")
---
your support means everything,

Bennet Campaign

\-------------- BEGIN FORWARDED MESSAGE --------------

[TO: dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cba2e8/134673f2/39f5ca0/6996ec31/1080707450/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.michaelbennet.com/Climate-X-Option?source=EM_PET_B2_052019_AC&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")
FROM: Michael Bennet
SUBJECT: I will not allow Mitch McConnell to be the earth's grim reaper
---
[As president, Michael Bennet would use executive action if Congress fails to act on climate change. Please chip in to send him to the debate: michaelbennet.com/debate](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cba2e8/134673f2/39f5ca0/6996ec30/1080707450/VEsF/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/emaildebate?refcode=EM_PET_B2_052019_AC&recurring=auto&amount=&amounts=10,35,50,100,250,500,1000")
* * *
---
Climate change is a global crisis that we must confront now.

The American people are demanding urgent action.

_So why isn 't Washington doing anything?_

Because bought-and-paid-for climate deniers like Donald Trump and Mitch McConnell put special interests ahead of our future.
[That's why I'm announcing an ambitious and actionable plan to push America to 100% clean, zero-emissions and drive economic growth for generations to come. If you're ready to take action, sign my pledge to join the fight.](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cba2e8/134673f2/39f5ca0/6996ec31/1080707450/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.michaelbennet.com/Climate-X-Option?source=EM_PET_B2_052019_AC&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")

| [SIGN THE PETITION ->](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cba2e8/134673f2/39f5ca0/6996ec31/1080707450/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.michaelbennet.com/Climate-X-Option?source=EM_PET_B2_052019_AC&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")
---
My plan for climate action will start on day 1. Here's what we'll do:
* Create an American Climate Council with seats for labor, farmers, ranchers, and other communities most directly affected by climate change.

* Rejoin the Paris climate agreement, because America will no longer take a back seat on the world stage.

* Set a deadline for Congress to pass climate legislation -- and if they refuse to act, I'll use my executive authority to take action myself.

My Climate X Option requires power providers to offer zero-emission energy to every household.

We must empower the American people to take action on a challenge that affects us all.

As President, I will sign comprehensive climate legislation into law by September 2021. If carbon polluters block progress, I will unlock the full authority of the Clean Air Act to ensure climate action.
---
[But I need your help to make it happen, because I can't take on this fight alone. Mitch McConnell must listen to the American people and if he doesn't, we must act without him. If you're ready to take action, sign on now.](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cba2e8/134673f2/39f5ca0/6996ec31/1080707450/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.michaelbennet.com/Climate-X-Option?source=EM_PET_B2_052019_AC&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")

| [SIGN THE PETITION ->](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cba2e8/134673f2/39f5ca0/6996ec31/1080707450/VEsB/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.michaelbennet.com/Climate-X-Option?source=EM_PET_B2_052019_AC&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")
---
Forward together,

Michael Bennet
---

| [As president, Michael Bennet would use executive action if Congress fails to act on climate change. Please chip in to send him to the debate: michaelbennet.com/debate](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cba2e8/134673f2/39f5ca0/6996ec30/1080707450/VEsO/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/emaildebate?refcode=EM_PET_B2_052019_AC&recurring=auto&amount=&amounts=10,35,50,100,250,500,1000")
